name: Jorogumo
description: The Jorogumo is a spider yōkai with the ability to change its appearance into that of a beautiful woman. They are typically found in caves, forests, and abandoned buildings. Jorogumo possess great intelligence and cunning, using their alluring form to ensnare unsuspecting men. They lure victims into their lairs with promises of love and riches, only to trap and devour them. Some legends claim that Jorogumo are created when a spider reaches 400 years of age, or when it is splattered with human blood. They are skilled in illusion, transformation, and manipulating spider silk for both trapping prey and combat. Their venom is highly potent and can paralyze or kill their victims. They are often depicted playing a biwa to further entice their prey. The Jorogumo represents the dangers of unchecked desire and the deceptive nature of beauty. They are a reminder that appearances can be deceiving, and that trusting strangers can lead to deadly consequences. The Jorogumo's story serves as a cautionary tale about the perils of succumbing to temptation and the importance of discerning true intentions.
subtitle: Spider Woman of Deception
appearance: Jorogumo typically appear as large spiders, often with a human-like face or torso. They possess the ability to transform into a beautiful and alluring woman, often dressed in fine clothing. In spider form, they can be as large as a human, with thick, hairy legs and sharp fangs. Their eyes are often described as piercing and captivating, regardless of their form.
habitat: Caves, forests, abandoned buildings, near waterfalls
behavior: Jorogumo are cunning and manipulative creatures. They lure unsuspecting men into their lairs through deception and promises of wealth or love. Once they have their prey trapped, they will devour them. They are often patient and will observe their victims for some time before making their move. They are also territorial and will defend their lairs fiercely.

keyMyths.title: The Waterfall Jorogumo
keyMyths.description: A Jorogumo near a waterfall lured men to their deaths with the sound of a biwa. A woodcutter discovered her lair and escaped, warning others of the danger.

sources.author: Shigeru Mizuki
sources.description: Manga series featuring various Japanese yōkai, including Jorogumo.
